Detailed Description

C object used to implement the java.lang.Thread class

uintptr_t  ref 
)

Implements the MONITOR_EXIT opcode

ref The object on which the monitor was taken

uintptr_t thread_create_main ( thread_t *  thread,
method_t *  run,
uintptr_t *  args 
)

Creates the main thread. This function doesn't return until the main thread has finished execution as it uses the current native/green thread instead of creating a new one

Parameters:
thread The main thread pre-allocated structure
run The main method
args A pointer to a reference to the arguments array
Returns:
The reference to an uncaught exception if the main method terminated abruptly or JNULL

void thread_init ( thread_t *  thread  ) 

Initializes all the thread-local structures required for normal operation of a thread including the thread's self reference

void thread_interrupt ( thread_t *  thread  ) 

Interrupts a thread. This function is used for implementing the java.lang.Thread.interrupt() method and can interrupt immediately the java.lang.Object.wait() and java.lang.Thread.sleep() methods but only asynchronously the java.lang.Thread.join() method

Parameters:
thread A pointer to the thread to be interrupted

Definition at line 1130 of file thread.c.

References thread_t::cond_int, thread_t::interrupted, native_cond_signal(), tm_lock(), and tm_unlock().

Referenced by java_lang_Thread_interrupt().

Referenced by java_lang_Thread_start(), and vm_run().

void thread_may_block ( void   ) 

Flags the calling thread as potentially blocked. This function must be called every time a thread might block for a long time in order to avoid livelocks. Two examples might be when the thread takes a lock or when it calls a blocking native method like recv(). When the thread has finished the blocking operation thread_resumes() must be called

Definition at line 1086 of file thread.c.

References self, and thread_self().

Referenced by thread_join(), thread_launch(), thread_sleep(), thread_wait(), and tm_lock().

bool thread_notify ( uintptr_t  ref,
bool  broadcast 
)

Implements the functionliaty required by java.lang.Object.notify()

Parameters:
ref The object used for the notification
broadcast If true, wake up all the threads waiting on this object, otherwise wake up only one thread
Returns:
true if everything goes well, false if the monitor associated with ref was not owned by thread. In the latter case an IllegalMonitorStateException should be thrown

Definition at line 1255 of file thread.c.

void thread_resumes ( void   ) 

Restores a thread to its steady state after a blocking operation. Before returning however this function checks if a global stop-the-world has been requested, if so it waits until it is asked to resume execution. This function must be always called after a blocking operation and coupled with a previous call to thread_may_block()

Definition at line 1100 of file thread.c.

References thread_manager_t::lock, native_mutex_lock(), native_mutex_unlock(), self, and thread_self().

Referenced by thread_join(), thread_launch(), thread_sleep(), thread_wait(), and tm_lock().

void thread_sleep ( int64_t  ms  ) 

Implements the functionality required by the java.lang.Thread.sleep() method, waiting for the number of milliseconds specified by ms

Parameters:
ms The number of milliseconds to wait

Definition at line 941 of file thread.c.

bool thread_wait ( uintptr_t  ref,
uint64_t  millis,
uint32_t  nanos 
)

Implements the functionality required by java.lang.Object.wait() and friends, waiting on an object until it is notified by notify() or notifyAll() or until a timeout occurs (if nanos or millis or both ar non-zero)

Parameters:
ref The object on which to wait
millis Number of milliseconds before a timeout occurs
nanos Number of nanoseconds before a timeout occurs
Returns:
true if everything goes well, false if the monitor associated with ref was not owned thread. In the latter case an IllegalMonitorStateException should be thrown

void tm_init ( void   ) 

Initialize the thread manager, this does not include the initialization of the Java monitors subsystem because the thread manager is initialized before the heap manager

Definition at line 392 of file thread.c.

References thread_manager_t::lock, native_key_create(), native_mutex_create(), and vm_fail().

Referenced by vm_run().

void tm_lock ( void   ) 

Try to gain the ownership of the machine-wide lock. This function will return only when the caller has the ownership of the lock in the meantime the calling thread is considered blocked. It is safe to call this function repeatedly from the same thread as long as tm_unlock() is called the same number of times to release the lock

Definition at line 659 of file thread.c.

References thread_manager_t::lock, native_mutex_lock(), thread_may_block(), and thread_resumes().
